name: Marketing Intake Form Integration

objects.role: Entry-point service that receives new and updated intake form submissions from Jotform via webhook
objects.state_description: Audit log of all received form submissions and updates, including timestamp, form ID, and payload
objects.behavior: When a new form submission or form update webhook arrives, forward the full payload to the intake-routing business logic object.

peers.relationship: Forward full form payload (new submission or update) immediately upon receipt

objects.role: Business logic object that processes intake form data and routes it to all downstream integration targets
objects.state_description: Tracks the most recent processed payload per form submission ID, including objectives, target audience, budget, timeline, and whether this is a new submission or an update
objects.behavior: When a new form submission arrives, extract project details (objectives, target audience, budget, timeline) and send the structured data simultaneously to hubspot-crm, asana-projects, and mailchimp-lists. When an update to an existing submission arrives, identify changed fields and send the updated data simultaneously to hubspot-crm, asana-projects, and mailchimp-lists so all platforms reflect the latest state. Never wait for confirmation from any downstream write service before considering the routing complete.

peers.relationship: Send structured contact and deal data for new submissions; send field updates for existing submissions
Send project task data for new submissions; send updated fields for existing submissions
Send subscriber and campaign data for new submissions; send updated fields for existing submissions

objects.role: Write service representing HubSpot CRM that records contact and deal information from intake form submissions
objects.state_description: Audit log of all CRM writes including contact records, deal records, and field updates with timestamps and submission IDs
objects.behavior: When a new submission payload arrives, create or update the corresponding contact and deal record in HubSpot with the provided project details. When an update payload arrives, patch the existing contact and deal record with the changed fields to keep the CRM current.

objects.role: Write service representing Asana project management that creates and updates project tasks from intake form submissions
objects.state_description: Audit log of all Asana writes including task creations, project assignments, and field updates with timestamps and submission IDs
objects.behavior: When a new submission payload arrives, create a new project task in Asana populated with objectives, target audience, budget, and timeline. When an update payload arrives, update the corresponding task fields to reflect the latest form data.

objects.role: Write service representing Mailchimp email marketing platform that manages subscriber records and campaign data from intake form submissions
objects.state_description: Audit log of all Mailchimp writes including subscriber additions, list assignments, and field updates with timestamps and submission IDs
objects.behavior: When a new submission payload arrives, add or update the subscriber record in Mailchimp with the relevant contact and campaign data. When an update payload arrives, patch the subscriber record with changed fields to keep Mailchimp aligned with the latest intake data.

steps: When a client or team member submits an intake form, the workflow captures the project objectives, target audience, budget, and timeline from the submission for downstream business use.

For each new intake submission, the workflow structures those project details and sends them in parallel to HubSpot, Asana, and Mailchimp so the CRM, project tracking, and email marketing records are created or updated from the same intake data.

If an existing intake form is updated, the workflow identifies which project details changed and pushes only those updates in parallel to HubSpot, Asana, and Mailchimp so each platform reflects the latest submission state and stakeholders stay informed.

modifications.intent: For the rest of this week, if someone only nudges the campaign dates on an intake that's already in flight, let's just hold that change and pick it up next Monday; if they changed anything else too, keep sending the update through like normal.

events.input: A new Jotform intake form submission has arrived. Submitted by Rachel Torres, Marketing Director at Greenfield Brands (rachel.torres@greenfieldbrand.com, +1-312-555-0192). Project details: Objectives: Launch a brand awareness campaign for the new product line 'EcoSpark'; Target Audience: Eco-conscious consumers aged 25-45 in the US Midwest; Budget: $45,000; Timeline: Campaign kick-off March 1, 2025, completion by June 30, 2025. Form submission ID: JF-20250201-0047.

expect.action: A new intake record is created or updated in HubSpot, Asana, and Mailchimp with the submitted project objectives, target audience, budget, and timeline for Greenfield Brands' EcoSpark campaign.
expect.reason: A new intake submission triggers the workflow to capture the project details and sync them to HubSpot, Asana, and Mailchimp.

events.input: A Jotform form update webhook has arrived for submission ID JF-20250201-0047, submitted by Rachel Torres, Marketing Director at Greenfield Brands (rachel.torres@greenfieldbrand.com, +1-312-555-0192). Updated project details: Budget revised from $45,000 to $52,000; Timeline updated: Campaign kick-off pushed to April 1, 2025, completion by July 31, 2025. All other fields remain unchanged.

expect.action: HubSpot, Asana, and Mailchimp are updated with the revised budget and campaign timeline for submission JF-20250201-0047, while the unchanged intake fields remain as previously stored.
expect.reason: An existing intake update causes the workflow to push only the changed fields to the connected business systems.

events.input: A Jotform intake form update webhook has arrived for submission ID JF-20250210-0118, submitted by Priya Nandakumar, Senior Marketing Manager at NorthPeak Home (priya.nandakumar@northpeakhome.com, +1-773-555-0148). Updated project details: Timeline updated from campaign kick-off May 5, 2025 and completion by August 15, 2025 to campaign kick-off May 12, 2025 and completion by August 22, 2025. Objectives remain: drive awareness for the new modular patio collection. Target audience remains: suburban homeowners aged 30-55 in Illinois, Wisconsin, and Minnesota. Budget remains: $38,000.

expect.action: HubSpot, Asana, and Mailchimp are updated with the revised campaign timeline for submission JF-20250210-0118, while the objectives, target audience, and budget remain unchanged.
expect.reason: An existing intake update with only timeline changes is synced to the connected systems under the baseline workflow.

events.input: A Jotform intake form update webhook has arrived for submission ID JF-20250211-0196, submitted by Elena Morales, Brand Manager at Harbor & Pine Foods (elena.morales@harborpinefoods.com, +1-414-555-0186). Updated project details: Timeline updated from campaign kick-off June 2, 2025 and completion by September 30, 2025 to campaign kick-off June 16, 2025 and completion by October 14, 2025. All other fields remain unchanged: Objectives: launch a seasonal awareness campaign for the new cold brew concentrate line; Target audience: grocery shoppers aged 22-40 across the US Midwest; Budget: $61,000.

expect.action: [suppressed by M001] The timeline-only update is held and not sent to HubSpot, Asana, or Mailchimp this week; no external record changes are produced yet.
expect.reason: M001 pauses updates that only nudge campaign dates on an in-flight intake until next Monday.

events.input: A new Jotform intake form submission has arrived. Submitted by Marcus Lee, Director of Growth at Cedar Lane Fitness (marcus.lee@cedarlanefitness.com, +1-216-555-0127). Project details: Objectives: promote the launch of a new recovery membership offer; Target Audience: existing gym members and lapsed members aged 25-50 in Ohio and Michigan; Budget: $24,000; Timeline: Campaign kick-off July 7, 2025, completion by September 1, 2025. Form submission ID: JF-20250212-0224.

expect.action: A new intake record is created or updated in HubSpot, Asana, and Mailchimp with Marcus Lee's project objectives, target audience, budget, and timeline for Cedar Lane Fitness.
expect.reason: A new intake submission triggers the workflow to sync the full intake data to the connected business systems.

events.input: A Jotform intake form update webhook has arrived for submission ID JF-20250209-0083, submitted by Danielle Brooks, Marketing Director at SummitTrail Apparel (danielle.brooks@summittrailapparel.com, +1-303-555-0175). Updated project details: Budget revised from $72,000 to $80,000; Timeline updated from campaign kick-off April 28, 2025 and completion by July 31, 2025 to campaign kick-off May 5, 2025 and completion by August 7, 2025. Objectives remain: launch a regional awareness push for the new all-weather hiking line. Target audience remains: outdoor enthusiasts aged 28-46 in Colorado, Utah, and Arizona.

expect.action: HubSpot, Asana, and Mailchimp are updated with the revised budget and campaign timeline for submission JF-20250209-0083, while the objectives and target audience remain unchanged.
expect.reason: An existing intake update with both budget and timeline changes is pushed through to the connected systems.

events.input: A Jotform intake form update webhook has arrived for submission ID JF-20250212-0231, submitted by Lauren Kim, VP of Marketing at BrightNest Interiors (lauren.kim@brightnestinteriors.com, +1-646-555-0119). Updated project details: Objectives revised from 'increase showroom foot traffic for the spring collection' to 'increase showroom foot traffic and consultation bookings for the spring collection'; Budget remains $57,000; Timeline remains campaign kick-off March 24, 2025, completion by June 30, 2025; Target audience remains homeowners aged 35-60 in the New York and New Jersey metro area.

expect.action: HubSpot, Asana, and Mailchimp are updated with the revised project objectives for submission JF-20250212-0231, while the budget, timeline, and target audience remain unchanged.
expect.reason: An existing intake update with a non-date field change is still processed normally because M001 only delays updates that change campaign dates and nothing else.

tools.description: Create or update the corresponding contact and deal record in HubSpot with intake submission data.
Patch the existing HubSpot contact and deal record with changed intake fields.
Create a new Asana project task populated with intake form details.
Update an existing Asana task with the latest intake form fields.
Add or update a Mailchimp subscriber record with contact and campaign data from the intake submission.
Patch an existing Mailchimp subscriber record with changed intake fields.

name: UTM Builder with Link Storage

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives form submissions from Jotform containing UTM parameters and website URL.
objects.state_description: Logs each incoming Jotform submission with its raw field values and timestamp.
objects.behavior: When a Jotform form submission arrives, forward all submitted fields (website URL, campaign ID, source, medium, name, term, content) to the utm-builder peer.

peers.relationship: Forward all UTM form fields when a new Jotform submission arrives

objects.role: Business logic object that constructs UTM-tagged URLs from submitted parameters and orchestrates URL shortening and storage.
objects.state_description: Tracks the current UTM parameter set, the generated full UTM-tagged URL, and the shortened URL for each request.
objects.behavior: When UTM parameters arrive (website URL, campaign ID, source, medium, name, term, content), encode them into a properly formatted UTM-tagged URL by appending query parameters (utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, utm_term, utm_content) to the base website URL using standard percent-encoding rules. Then send the full UTM-tagged URL to bitly-shortener to obtain a shortened link. When the shortened URL is returned from bitly-shortener, send both the full UTM-tagged URL and the shortened URL to marketing-links-sheet for storage.

peers.relationship: Send the full UTM-tagged URL when it has been constructed, requesting a shortened version
Send the full UTM-tagged URL and shortened URL when both are ready for storage

objects.role: Read service object that calls the Bitly API to generate a shortened URL from a long URL and returns it.
objects.behavior: When a long URL is received, call the Bitly API to shorten it and return the shortened URL to the caller. When answering queries, call the `bitly_shortener_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Write service object that stores the full UTM-tagged URL and shortened URL into the Marketing Links Google Sheet under the UTM Tracker tab.
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all UTM link records written, including full URL, shortened URL, and timestamp of each write.
objects.behavior: When a record containing a full UTM-tagged URL and a shortened URL is received, append a new row to the `UTM Tracker` tab of the `Marketing Links` Google Sheet with the full URL, shortened URL, and current timestamp.

steps: A user submits a Jotform request containing a website URL plus UTM details for campaign ID, source, medium, name, term, and content, and the workflow captures all of those submitted values for processing.

The workflow builds a tracking URL from the submitted website address by appending standard UTM query parameters for source, medium, campaign, term, and content, applying normal percent-encoding so the final link is properly formatted.
After the full UTM-tagged URL is created, the workflow sends it to Bitly to generate a shortened version of that same link and waits for the shortened URL to be returned.

Once both versions of the link are available, the workflow records the full UTM-tagged URL and the Bitly short URL in a Google Sheet used for marketing link tracking, along with the current timestamp.

modifications.intent: Let’s stop tacking on the term and content pieces when they’re basically not filled in.

events.input: A Jotform form submission arrives with the following UTM parameters: website URL: https://www.example.com/landing-page, campaign ID: CAMP-2024-Q4, source: google, medium: cpc, name: holiday-promo, term: running-shoes, content: banner-ad-v2.

expect.action: A UTM-tagged marketing link is generated from the submitted website URL and UTM values, then a shortened Bitly link is created, and both links plus the current timestamp are appended to the Google Sheet used for marketing link tracking.
expect.reason: The workflow captures the submitted UTM details, builds the tracking URL, shortens it with Bitly, and records both URLs in Google Sheets with a timestamp.

events.input: Jotform submission received at submission_id JF-2001 with website URL https://www.example.com/spring-sale, campaign ID CAMP-2024-SPRING, source google, medium cpc, name spring-launch, term , content .

expect.action: A UTM-tagged marketing link is generated from the submitted website URL and UTM values, then a shortened Bitly link is created, and both links plus the current timestamp are appended to the Google Sheet used for marketing link tracking.
expect.reason: The workflow processes each Jotform submission by building the tracking URL, shortening it in Bitly, and logging the resulting links in Google Sheets.

events.input: Jotform submission received at submission_id JF-2002 with website URL https://www.example.com/summer-collection, campaign ID CAMP-2024-SUMMER, source instagram, medium paid-social, name creator-push, term , content .

expect.action: A UTM-tagged marketing link is generated from the submitted website URL and UTM values, but term and content are omitted from the final tracking URL because the modification suppresses empty term/content parameters; the resulting long URL is shortened in Bitly, and both links plus the current timestamp are appended to the Google Sheet used for marketing link tracking.
expect.reason: Modification M001 changes the URL builder to stop adding term and content when those fields are blank, while the rest of the tracking and logging workflow remains the same.

events.input: Jotform submission received at submission_id JF-2003 with website URL https://www.example.com/back-to-school, campaign ID CAMP-2024-BTS, source newsletter, medium email, name august-send, term parents, content hero-banner.

expect.action: A UTM-tagged marketing link is generated from the submitted website URL and UTM values, then a shortened Bitly link is created, and both links plus the current timestamp are appended to the Google Sheet used for marketing link tracking.
expect.reason: Although M001 is active, this submission includes term and content values, so the modified URL builder still includes them and the downstream Bitly and Google Sheets steps proceed normally.

events.input: Jotform submission received at submission_id JF-2004 with website URL https://www.example.com/fall-preview, campaign ID CAMP-2024-FALL, source facebook, medium paid-social, name teaser-wave, term n/a, content n/a.

expect.action: A UTM-tagged marketing link is generated from the submitted website URL and UTM values, but term and content are omitted from the final tracking URL because the modification suppresses empty or placeholder term/content values; the resulting long URL is shortened in Bitly, and both links plus the current timestamp are appended to the Google Sheet used for marketing link tracking.
expect.reason: With M001 active, non-filled term/content values such as n/a are treated as not filled in, so they are excluded from the tracking URL before shortening and logging.

events.input: Jotform submission received at submission_id JF-2005 with website URL https://www.example.com/winter-launch, campaign ID CAMP-2024-WINTER, source bing, medium cpc, name cold-weather-promo, term insulated boots, content text-ad-variant-b.

expect.action: A UTM-tagged marketing link is generated from the submitted website URL and UTM values, then a shortened Bitly link is created, and both links plus the current timestamp are appended to the Google Sheet used for marketing link tracking.
expect.reason: The submission contains populated term and content values, so the modified builder includes them and the standard Bitly and Google Sheets outputs are produced.

tools.description: Retrieve reference data from bitly shortener data. Read service object that calls the Bitly API to generate a shortened URL from a long URL and returns it.
Append a new row to the Marketing Links Google Sheet in the UTM Tracker tab with the full UTM-tagged URL, shortened URL, and timestamp.

name: Document Approval Workflow

objects.role: Entry-point service representing the Jotform upload form that receives document submissions from requesters
objects.state_description: Tracks incoming form submissions including document files and requester contact details
objects.behavior: When a new document submission arrives via the Jotform form, forward the submission payload (document, requester email, requester name, submission metadata) to the approval-policy object.

peers.relationship: Forward full submission payload including document and requester details when a new form submission arrives

event_sources: webhook: form submission with document upload

objects.role: Business logic object that enforces the document approval workflow, routing documents for review and processing approval decisions
objects.state_description: Tracks each document submission with its current status (pending, approved, rejected), requester details, approver assignment, and decision timestamp
objects.behavior: When a new document submission arrives, send the document record to pending-submissions for storage and send an approval request notification to approver-notifications simultaneously. When an approval or rejection decision event arrives (from the approval-action-handler), evaluate the decision: if approved, mark the document as approved; if rejected, mark it as rejected. Then send the decision notification to requester-notifications.

peers.relationship: Send document record with requester details and status 'pending' when a new submission arrives
Send approval request email with document details and approve/reject action links when a new submission arrives
Send approval decision email with outcome (approved or rejected) to the requester when a decision is received

objects.role: Write service representing the Pending Submissions table in the Document Approvals Airtable base that stores submitted documents
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all document submissions with requester info, document content, submission timestamp, and approval status
objects.behavior: When a document record is received, store it in the Pending Submissions table in the Document Approvals Airtable base with status, requester details, document reference, and timestamp.

objects.role: Write service representing the email notification system that sends approval request emails to the approver
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all approval request emails sent, including document reference and timestamp
objects.behavior: When an approval request message is received, send an email to the designated approver containing the document details and embedded approve and reject action buttons linking back to the workflow.

objects.role: Entry-point service representing the approve/reject button click mechanism in the approver email that captures the approver's decision
objects.state_description: Tracks approval action events received including document ID, decision (approve or reject), and approver identity
objects.behavior: When an approver clicks the approve or reject button in the notification email, forward the decision event including document reference and decision value to the approval-policy object.

peers.relationship: Forward approval decision (approve or reject) with document reference when the approver clicks a decision button

event_sources: webhook: approver clicks approve or reject button in email

objects.role: Write service representing the email notification system that sends approval decision emails to requesters
objects.state_description: Audit trail of all decision notification emails sent to requesters, including document reference, decision, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a decision notification message is received, send an email to the requester with the approval outcome (approved or rejected) and any relevant details about their submitted document.

steps: A requester submits a document through a Jotform upload form, providing the file along with their email address, name, and submission metadata. The workflow receives this submission and starts the document approval process.

After submission, the workflow records the document in Airtable as a pending approval item, storing its status, requester details, document reference, and submission timestamp. This creates the review record used for the approval process.

When a new document is submitted, the workflow sends an approval request email to the designated approver. This notification is triggered alongside storing the submission, so the document is both logged and routed for review at the same time.

The approver reviews the email and selects either Approve or Reject using the action buttons included in the message. That decision, together with the document reference, is sent back into the workflow for evaluation.

Once the workflow receives the approver's decision, it marks the document as approved if the decision is approval, or rejected if the decision is rejection, and then sends an email to the requester with the outcome and relevant details about their submitted document.

modifications.intent: Let anything that comes in after 6 PM wait and go out first thing the next business morning instead of nudging the approver right away.

events.input: A new document submission has arrived from the Jotform upload form. Requester: Maria Santos, maria.santos@example.com. Document: 'Q3_Budget_Proposal.pdf' (uploaded file reference: jotform-upload-abc123). Submission ID: JF-20240315-001. Submitted at: 2024-03-15T10:30:00Z.

expect.action: A new pending approval record is created in the Document Approvals Airtable base's Pending Submissions table for submission ID JF-20240315-001, with document 'Q3_Budget_Proposal.pdf' (reference: jotform-upload-abc123), requester Maria Santos, requester email maria.santos@example.com, status 'pending approval', and the submission timestamp 2024-03-15T10:30:00Z. An approval request email is sent to David Chen at david.chen@example.com with the document details and Approve and Reject action buttons.
expect.reason: A Jotform document submission for a normal-priority general document is logged as pending in Airtable and routed by email to the designated tier_1 approver.

events.input: The approver has clicked the Approve button in the notification email for document 'Q3_Budget_Proposal.pdf' (submission ID: JF-20240315-001, document reference: jotform-upload-abc123). Decision: approved. Approver: David Chen, david.chen@example.com.

expect.action: The document approval record for submission ID JF-20240315-001 / document reference jotform-upload-abc123 is updated in the Document Approvals Airtable base so the status is approved. An outcome email is sent to the requester stating that 'Q3_Budget_Proposal.pdf' was approved, including the relevant submitted document details.
expect.reason: When the approver submits an approved decision, the workflow marks the document approved and notifies the requester by email.

events.input: A new document submission has arrived from the Jotform upload form. Requester: Lena Patel, lena.patel@example.com. Document: 'Vendor_Onboarding_Checklist.pdf' (uploaded file reference: jotform-upload-voc771). Submission ID: JF-20240408-014. Submitted at: 2024-04-08T16:20:00Z.

expect.action: A new pending approval record is created in the Document Approvals Airtable base's Pending Submissions table for submission ID JF-20240408-014, with document 'Vendor_Onboarding_Checklist.pdf' (reference: jotform-upload-voc771), requester Lena Patel, requester email lena.patel@example.com, status 'pending approval', and the submission timestamp 2024-04-08T16:20:00Z. An approval request email is sent to David Chen at david.chen@example.com with the document details and Approve and Reject action buttons.
expect.reason: A Jotform document submission received before the after-hours cutoff follows baseline behavior: it is stored in Airtable and immediately emailed to the designated tier_1 approver.

events.input: A new document submission has arrived from the Jotform upload form. Requester: Omar Hassan, omar.hassan@example.com. Document: 'Office_Lease_Renewal.docx' (uploaded file reference: jotform-upload-olr552). Submission ID: JF-20240409-027. Submitted at: 2024-04-09T18:35:00Z.

expect.action: A new pending approval record is created in the Document Approvals Airtable base's Pending Submissions table for submission ID JF-20240409-027, with document 'Office_Lease_Renewal.docx' (reference: jotform-upload-olr552), requester Omar Hassan, requester email omar.hassan@example.com, status 'pending approval', and the submission timestamp 2024-04-09T18:35:00Z. [deferred by M001] The approval request email to David Chen is held and not produced at submission time; it is scheduled to go out first thing the next business morning instead of immediately after the 6 PM cutoff.
expect.reason: M001 applies because this new submission arrived after 6 PM, so the record is still created in Airtable but the approver email is deferred until the next business morning.

events.input: The approver has clicked the Reject button in the notification email for document 'Master_Services_Agreement.pdf' (submission ID: JF-20240409-011, document reference: jotform-upload-msa118). Decision: rejected. Approver: David Chen, david.chen@example.com. Action captured at: 2024-04-09T11:10:00Z.

expect.action: The document approval record for submission ID JF-20240409-011 / document reference jotform-upload-msa118 is updated in the Document Approvals Airtable base so the status is rejected. An outcome email is sent to the requester stating that 'Master_Services_Agreement.pdf' was rejected, including the relevant submitted document details.
expect.reason: When the approver submits a rejected decision, the workflow marks the document rejected and notifies the requester by email. [M001 produces no observable difference on this input]

events.input: A new document submission has arrived from the Jotform upload form. Requester: Priya Nair, priya.nair@example.com. Document: 'Quarterly_Compliance_Attestation.pdf' (uploaded file reference: jotform-upload-qca903). Submission ID: JF-20240410-006. Submitted at: 2024-04-10T09:15:00Z.

expect.action: A new pending approval record is created in the Document Approvals Airtable base's Pending Submissions table for submission ID JF-20240410-006, with document 'Quarterly_Compliance_Attestation.pdf' (reference: jotform-upload-qca903), requester Priya Nair, requester email priya.nair@example.com, status 'pending approval', and the submission timestamp 2024-04-10T09:15:00Z. An approval request email is sent to David Chen at david.chen@example.com with the document details and Approve and Reject action buttons.
expect.reason: M001 is active but its after-hours condition is not met because this submission arrived before 6 PM, so baseline behavior applies: Airtable logging plus immediate email to the designated approver. [M001 condition not met: submission was not after 6 PM]

events.input: A new document submission has arrived from the Jotform upload form. Requester: Miguel Alvarez, miguel.alvarez@example.com. Document: 'Travel_Reimbursement_Supporting_Docs.zip' (uploaded file reference: jotform-upload-trs447). Submission ID: JF-20240410-021. Submitted at: 2024-04-10T19:05:00Z.

expect.action: A new pending approval record is created in the Document Approvals Airtable base's Pending Submissions table for submission ID JF-20240410-021, with document 'Travel_Reimbursement_Supporting_Docs.zip' (reference: jotform-upload-trs447), requester Miguel Alvarez, requester email miguel.alvarez@example.com, status 'pending approval', and the submission timestamp 2024-04-10T19:05:00Z. [deferred by M001] The approval request email to David Chen is held and not produced at submission time; it is scheduled to go out first thing the next business morning instead of immediately after the 6 PM cutoff.
expect.reason: M001 applies because this new submission arrived after 6 PM, so the submission is recorded in Airtable now and the approver notification is delayed until the next business morning.

tools.description: Create a new record in the Pending Submissions table in the Document Approvals Airtable base for a submitted document.
Send an approval request email to the designated approver with document details and approve/reject action links.
Send an approval decision email to the requester with the outcome and relevant document details.
Transmit a new document submission payload from the form entry point to the approval workflow for processing.
Write a submitted document record into the Pending Submissions table in the Document Approvals Airtable base.
Retrieve reference data from approval authority data. Reference data mapping document submissions or requester attributes to the designated approver or approval routing rules.

tools.description: Update an existing record in the Pending Submissions table to reflect the approver's decision and related status details.

name: Customer Interview Signup Automation

objects.role: Entry-point service object for new interview events from the Product Research shared Google Calendar.
objects.state_description: Initially empty; records each incoming new-calendar-event payload as an audit trail of received triggers.
objects.behavior: When a new interview event is received from the Product Research shared Google Calendar, record the event payload and treat it as a newly scheduled customer interview trigger.

peers.relationship: Forward newly scheduled interview event details when a calendar interview is created

event_sources: google calendar: event created on Product Research shared calendar

objects.role: Dual-role service object for posting interview announcements to Slack and receiving Product Manager signup actions from Slack.
objects.state_description: Initially empty; records outbound announcement payloads and inbound signup action events as an audit trail.
objects.behavior: When asked to post an announcement, post to the #user-research-interviews channel. When Product Manager signup action arrives, forward it to interview-signup-rules via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward Product Manager signup action details when a signup action is received from Slack

objects.role: Write service object for updating interview events in Google Calendar with attendee information.
objects.state_description: Initially empty; records requested calendar event updates, including which Product Manager should be added to which interview event.
objects.behavior: When asked to update an interview event, record the requested change to include the specified Product Manager as an attendee on the corresponding Google Calendar event.

objects.role: Write service object for creating and updating interview records in the User Research Interviews Airtable base.
objects.state_description: Initially empty; records requested Airtable writes for scheduled interview creation and attendee updates in the Scheduled Interviews table.
objects.behavior: When asked to create a scheduled interview record, record the customer details and status scheduled in the User Research Interviews Airtable base, Scheduled Interviews table. When asked to update an interview record, record the Product Manager attendee in the corresponding Scheduled Interviews table row.

objects.role: Business logic object that applies the rules for publishing interview opportunities and assigning Product Manager attendees to scheduled interviews.
objects.state_description: Tracks known scheduled interviews by calendar event identity, customer details, current status, Slack announcement context, and assigned Product Manager attendee when one signs up.
objects.behavior: When a newly scheduled interview arrives, extract the interview date, time, and customer details, mark the interview as scheduled, and prepare a notification announcing the opportunity for Product Managers to sign up. When a Product Manager signup action arrives, match it to the corresponding upcoming interview, determine whether the interview is still available for signup, and assign that Product Manager as the attendee. When an interview is newly scheduled, send to airtable-records and slack-signup-action. When a Product Manager is assigned, send to google-calendar and airtable-records.

peers.relationship: Send scheduled interview details for record creation and assigned attendee details for record update
Send interview announcement details for posting when a new interview is scheduled
Send attendee update details when a Product Manager signs up for an interview

steps: When a new customer interview is scheduled on the shared Google Calendar used for product research, the workflow treats that calendar event as the trigger for a newly scheduled interview. It uses the event details provided there, including the interview date, time, and customer information.

The workflow creates a scheduled interview record in Airtable using the customer details from the Google Calendar event and marks its status as scheduled. This happens whenever a newly scheduled interview is received.

After identifying a newly scheduled interview, the workflow posts an announcement in Slack to notify the team that a new interview opportunity is available for Product Managers to sign up for. The announcement is prepared from the interview date, time, and customer details.

When a Product Manager signs up from the Slack announcement, the workflow matches that signup to the corresponding upcoming interview and checks whether the session is still available for signup. If it is available, that Product Manager is assigned as the attendee.

Once a Product Manager has been assigned to the interview, the workflow updates the corresponding Google Calendar event to include that person as an attendee. This calendar update only occurs after a valid signup has been accepted.

After assigning a Product Manager to the interview, the workflow updates the corresponding Airtable interview record to add that attendee. This record update happens only when a signup has been matched to an upcoming interview and accepted.

modifications.intent: For the rest of this week, if a new interview gets put on the calendar kind of late in the day, just don't push it out to Slack right away.

events.input: A new event is created on the Product Research shared Google Calendar for a customer interview with Priya Shah from Northstar Health on September 18, 2026 from 2:00 PM to 2:45 PM PT. The event includes customer details: name Priya Shah, company Northstar Health, email priya.shah@northstarhealth.com, and notes 'Discuss onboarding experience and reporting workflow pain points.'

expect.action: A new scheduled interview record is created in Airtable for Priya Shah at Northstar Health for September 18, 2026 from 2:00 PM to 2:45 PM PT, including priya.shah@northstarhealth.com, the interview notes, and status set to "scheduled". A Slack announcement is posted in #user-research-interviews notifying the team that a new interview with Priya Shah from Northstar Health on September 18, 2026 from 2:00 PM to 2:45 PM PT is available for a Product Manager to sign up for.
expect.reason: When a new customer interview is created on the shared Google Calendar, the workflow records it in Airtable as scheduled and announces the open interview in Slack for Product Managers to claim.

events.input: A Slack interactive signup action is submitted from the #user-research-interviews channel by Product Manager Alex Kim to sign up as the attendee for the upcoming interview with Priya Shah from Northstar Health on September 18, 2026 from 2:00 PM to 2:45 PM PT.

expect.action: The matching upcoming interview for Priya Shah from Northstar Health is assigned to Product Manager Alex Kim. The corresponding Google Calendar interview event is updated to add Alex Kim as an attendee. The corresponding Airtable interview record is updated to add Alex Kim as the assigned attendee.
expect.reason: When a Slack signup is submitted for an interview that is still available, the workflow accepts the signup, assigns that Product Manager, and updates both Google Calendar and Airtable with the attendee.

events.input: A new event is created on the Product Research shared Google Calendar for a customer interview with Elena Garcia from Harbor Fitness on September 22, 2026 from 1:00 PM to 1:45 PM PT. Calendar event ID: gcal-int-2201. The event includes customer details: name Elena Garcia, company Harbor Fitness, email elena.garcia@harborfitness.com, and notes 'Discuss first-week setup experience and confusion around team permissions.'

expect.action: A new scheduled interview record is created in Airtable for Elena Garcia at Harbor Fitness for September 22, 2026 from 1:00 PM to 1:45 PM PT, including elena.garcia@harborfitness.com, the interview notes, calendar event ID gcal-int-2201, and status set to "scheduled". A Slack announcement is posted in #user-research-interviews notifying the team that a new interview with Elena Garcia from Harbor Fitness on September 22, 2026 from 1:00 PM to 1:45 PM PT is available for a Product Manager to sign up for.
expect.reason: When a new customer interview is created on the shared Google Calendar, the workflow records it in Airtable as scheduled and announces the open interview in Slack for Product Managers to claim.

events.input: A new event is created on the Product Research shared Google Calendar for a customer interview with Marcus Lee from Pine Ridge Logistics on September 23, 2026 from 5:30 PM to 6:00 PM PT. Calendar event ID: gcal-int-2202. The event includes customer details: name Marcus Lee, company Pine Ridge Logistics, email marcus.lee@pineridgelogistics.com, and notes 'Review warehouse reporting delays and mobile scanner workflow issues.'

expect.action: A new scheduled interview record is created in Airtable for Marcus Lee at Pine Ridge Logistics for September 23, 2026 from 5:30 PM to 6:00 PM PT, including marcus.lee@pineridgelogistics.com, the interview notes, calendar event ID gcal-int-2202, and status set to "scheduled". [suppressed by M001] The Slack announcement that would normally be posted in #user-research-interviews for Product Manager signup is not produced now.
expect.reason: A new calendar interview is still recorded in Airtable, but M001 suppresses the normal Slack announcement for interviews added late in the day during the rest of this week.

events.input: A Slack interactive signup action is submitted from the #user-research-interviews channel by Product Manager Nina Patel to sign up as the attendee for the upcoming interview with Elena Garcia from Harbor Fitness on September 22, 2026 from 1:00 PM to 1:45 PM PT. Slack message ts: 1727026500.441200. Slack action id: signup_interview. Calendar event ID: gcal-int-2201. Product Manager email: nina.patel@company.com.

expect.action: The upcoming interview with Elena Garcia from Harbor Fitness, calendar event ID gcal-int-2201, is assigned to Product Manager Nina Patel. The corresponding Google Calendar event gcal-int-2201 is updated to add nina.patel@company.com as an attendee. The corresponding Airtable interview record for Elena Garcia is updated to add Nina Patel as the assigned attendee.
expect.reason: When a Slack signup is submitted for a matching interview that is still available, the workflow accepts the signup and updates both Google Calendar and Airtable with the assigned attendee; [M001 produces no observable difference on this input]

events.input: A new event is created on the Product Research shared Google Calendar for a customer interview with Sophie Nguyen from ClearCurrent Energy on September 24, 2026 from 3:00 PM to 3:45 PM PT. Calendar event ID: gcal-int-2203. The event includes customer details: name Sophie Nguyen, company ClearCurrent Energy, email sophie.nguyen@clearcurrentenergy.com, and notes 'Discuss dashboard adoption, export usage, and where billing alerts are getting missed.'

expect.action: A new scheduled interview record is created in Airtable for Sophie Nguyen at ClearCurrent Energy for September 24, 2026 from 3:00 PM to 3:45 PM PT, including sophie.nguyen@clearcurrentenergy.com, the interview notes, calendar event ID gcal-int-2203, and status set to "scheduled". A Slack announcement is posted in #user-research-interviews notifying the team that a new interview with Sophie Nguyen from ClearCurrent Energy on September 24, 2026 from 3:00 PM to 3:45 PM PT is available for a Product Manager to sign up for.
expect.reason: The interview was created during the period when M001 exists, but the late-in-the-day condition is not met, so baseline behavior applies: the workflow records it in Airtable and posts the signup announcement in Slack. [M001 condition not met: this interview was not put on the calendar late in the day]

events.input: A Slack interactive signup action is submitted from the #user-research-interviews channel by Product Manager Jordan Ramirez to sign up as the attendee for the upcoming interview with Sophie Nguyen from ClearCurrent Energy on September 24, 2026 from 3:00 PM to 3:45 PM PT. Slack message ts: 1727119200.552900. Slack action id: signup_interview. Calendar event ID: gcal-int-2203. Product Manager email: jordan.ramirez@company.com.

expect.action: The upcoming interview with Sophie Nguyen from ClearCurrent Energy, calendar event ID gcal-int-2203, is assigned to Product Manager Jordan Ramirez. The corresponding Google Calendar event gcal-int-2203 is updated to add jordan.ramirez@company.com as an attendee. The corresponding Airtable interview record for Sophie Nguyen is updated to add Jordan Ramirez as the assigned attendee.
expect.reason: When a Slack signup is submitted for a matching interview that is still available, the workflow accepts the signup and updates both Google Calendar and Airtable with the assigned attendee; [M001 produces no observable difference on this input]

tools.description: Post an interview announcement message to the #user-research-interviews Slack channel.
Update a Google Calendar interview event to add the specified Product Manager as an attendee.
Create a scheduled interview record in the User Research Interviews Airtable base.
Update an existing scheduled interview record in Airtable to add the Product Manager attendee.
